ZENITH ENERGY LTD.
("Zenith" or the "Company")
Payment of 2029 Multi-Currency Bond Coupons
Zenith Energy Ltd. ("
Zenith
" or the "
Company
") (
LSE: ZEN; OSE: ZENA; XSAT: ZENA SDR
), the listed international energy production and development company, is pleased to announce that it has recently made coupon payments in full in respect of the following multi-currency Euro Medium Term Notes the Company has issued on the Vienna MTF of the Vienna Stock Exchange:
? Zenith 2029 (ISIN: XS2994528912)
? Zenith 2029 (ISIN: XS2994529134)
? Zenith 2029 (ISIN: XS2994529050)
Further Information
:
Zenith Energy Ltd
Andrea Cattaneo, Chief Executive Officer
Tel: +1 (587) 315 1279
E: info@zenithenergy.ca
Notes to Editors
:
Zenith Energy Ltd. is a revenue generating, independent energy company with energy production, exploration and development assets in North Africa, the US and Europe. The Company is listed on the London Stock Exchange Main Market (LSE: ZEN), the Euronext Growth of the Oslo Stock Exchange (OSE: ZENA) and on the Spotlight Stock Market in Sweden (XSAT: ZENA SDR)
.
Zenith's strategic focus is on pursuing development opportunities through the development of proven revenue generating energy production assets, as well as low-risk exploration activities in assets with existing production.
For more information, please visit: www.zenithenergy.ca
Twitter: @zenithenergyltd

latest.title: Commencement of Construction: 7 MWp Solar Plants
latest.content:
Today 07:12
RNS Number : 4806T
Zenith Energy Ltd
18 February 2026
February 18, 2026
ZENITH ENERGY LTD.
("
Zenith
" or the "
Company
")
Commencement of Construction: 7 MWp Solar Plants
Zenith Energy Ltd. ("
Zenith
" or the "
Company
") (
LSE: ZEN; OSE: ZENA; XSAT: ZENA SDR
), the listed international energy production and development company, is pleased to announce, through its wholly-owned Italian subsidiary WESOLAR S.R.L. ("
WESOLAR
"), that it has commenced construction-phase activities for the first assets within its Italian solar pipeline. Zenith has selected three projects in the Puglia region of Italy, totaling 7 MWp, for construction: Andria-1, Andria-3 and Andria-4 (together, the "
Under Construction Portfolio
" or "
UCP
").
Key construction and development milestones
·
The layouts for all ground-mounted plants within the Under Construction Portfolio have been completed.
·
All grid-connection applications have been submitted.
·
The purchase of panels and construction costs for the
UCP
is expected to be approximately EUR 3,150,000, together with land purchase price of EUR 720,000, for a total expected cost of EUR 3,870,000.
·
Upon completion of the construction, the Company estimates gross proceeds of approximately EUR 14.8 million, in the first 10 years.
·
The duration of the production of this plant is expected for about 30 years.
·
The Company has initiated a tender process for construction and is in advanced discussions with banking counterparties to secure construction financing, as announced in the Company's regulatory news release dated November 20, 2025.
·
Subject to final approvals and customary conditions, the Company anticipates that financing would cover approximately 80% of total land acquisition and construction costs, with the remaining approximately 20% funded through an equity contribution by WESOLAR.
·
The Company has scheduled the start of physical construction of the first plants for July 2026.
·
The sale value of these 3 properties, once in production, is estimated to be 9.1 million Euro.
New Acquisition of two ground-mounted PV projects in Puglia
To maintain pipeline momentum and replace assets that have progressed into construction, Zenith, via WESOLAR, has also acquired two new ground-mounted photovoltaic ("
PV
") projects in the Puglia region of Italy (the "
Acquisitions
").
Overview of the Acquisitions
·
The Company has acquired two new ground-mounted PV projects located in the Puglia region of Italy.
·
The projects are currently at development stage and are located on land parcels with surface areas of approximately 4 hectares and 2 hectares, respectively.
·
The projects are expected to have a combined installed capacity of approximately 5 MWp.
·
The consideration for the land is EUR 779,000, conditional upon the achievement of all necessary permits to reach Ready-to-Build status
Strategy overview
The progression of projects from development to construction provides evidence of the tangible progress of Zenith's solar strategy in Italy and further enhances the value of the Company's development pipeline.
In the solar energy industry, a "pipeline" refers to the aggregate portfolio of development projects being actively advanced toward construction. Zenith's pipeline is composed of projects progressing through design, permitting and grid-connection milestones, moving toward Ready-to-Build ("
RtB
") status.
This portfolio carries a tangible market value, which typically increases as projects mature and are de-risked through successive development stages. As previously announced in the Company's regulatory news release dated December 4, 2025, Zenith received the results of an independent valuation of its existing solar energy development pipeline (the "
Pipeline
"). At that time, the Pipeline totaled 110.5 MWp and was independently valued at EUR 27,500,000. This evaluation will need to be updated in due course.
With further acquisitions since then and the addition of the two Puglia PV projects described in this announcement (approximately 5 MWp), the Company's solar portfolio increases to approximately 125.5 MWp. No updated independent valuation has been published for the enlarged portfolio.
Zenith intends to maintain a balanced approach across its Italian portfolio, progressing projects toward RtB status while selectively assessing opportunities to monetize portions of the development pipeline with professional counterparties and to construct selected assets to establish tangible and recurring electricity production revenue.

Notes to the table:
·
Lazio Agrivoltaic Project 2: Capacity increased from 7 MWp to 8 MWp.
·
Piedmont Agrivoltaic Development Project: Capacity Increased from 18 MWp to 19 MWp.
·
Puglia PV Development: Capacity reduced from 12 MWp to 10 MWp due to bureaucratic requirements, to enable faster processing of the permitting application.
Comment from Andrea Cattaneo, Chief Executive Officer:
"
The commencement of construction-phase activities for our first Italian solar assets marks an important step in the execution of Zenith's renewables strategy. With three projects totaling 7 MWp now progressing through the tender and financing process, and with additional development land secured in Puglia to keep the pipeline flowing, we believe we are building a portfolio that can deliver scalable and recurring electricity production revenues over time. We look forward to providing further updates as we progress toward physical construction and as our financing discussions advance
."
Further Information
:
Zenith Energy Ltd
Andrea Cattaneo, Chief Executive Officer
Tel: +1 (587) 315 1279
E: info@zenithenergy.ca

The Company is listed on the London Stock Exchange Main Market (LSE: ZEN), the Euronext Growth of the Oslo Stock Exchange (OSE: ZENA) and on the Spotlight Stock Market in Sweden (XSAT: ZENA SDR)
.
Zenith's strategic focus is on pursuing development opportunities through the development of proven revenue generating energy production assets, as well as low-risk exploration activities in assets with existing production.
For more information, please visit: www.zenithenergy.ca
Twitter: @zenithenergyltd
LinkedIn: https://bit.ly/3A5PRJb

company_30s.what_they_do: Zenith Energy Ltd. builds and operates small‑scale solar farms, mainly 7‑MWp plants, and sells the generated power under long‑term contracts. They also manage a portfolio of renewable projects across the UK and Norway.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global push for clean energy makes solar a key growth area. Zenith’s focus on low‑cost, modular plants lets it compete with larger utilities while keeping capital outlays manageable. Their projects sit in regions with strong government incentives, giving them a stable revenue base.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in a rebuilding phase. After a series of bond maturities and a large options grant, it has just started construction on a new plant and secured a private placement to shore up cash. The shares are trading at a very low price, reflecting the recent cash‑burn and debt obligations.

rns_narrative: Zenith’s latest filings show a steady stream of operational milestones: a new 7‑MWp solar plant has begun construction, a private placement has injected fresh capital, and a bond coupon payment is due soon. These moves signal that the company is actively working to generate cash flow and reduce leverage.

The narrative clusters into three themes: 1) **Cash‑flow management** – bond payments and private placement; 2) **Operational expansion** – plant construction and project acquisition; 3) **Governance strengthening** – legal team expansion and independent valuation.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism about a slow but steady rebuild
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders note that the options grant shows management’s confidence and the new plant could start delivering revenue within a year.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others point to the company’s heavy debt load, the uncertainty of new contracts, and the risk that the new plant might not meet projected output.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Initially indifferent, sentiment is slowly warming as operational dates are announced, but no large volume spikes have yet occurred.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, indicating no mainstream buzz. This silence is a green flag for contrarian play – a surprise event could trigger a jump.

near_term_catalysts: The upcoming bond coupon payment on March 2, 2026 could lift cash reserves and improve liquidity.
Completion of the 7‑MWp plant’s construction (expected mid‑2026) would start revenue generation and may validate the project’s economics.
The private placement in February should provide a cash cushion, making the company less reliant on debt.
Any positive independent valuation report or a new long‑term power purchase agreement would add credibility.
expect_next.watch_for: Key confirmation signals include: a) a sharp uptick in daily trading volume that breaks the 50‑day moving average; b) a clear upward move past the 20‑day low, indicating a breakout from the crash zone; c) a positive earnings or cash‑flow report that shows the new plant is on schedule.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early post‑crash phase. There is no sign of a recent capitulation wave, so a mean‑reversion bounce would need a clear catalyst rather than a purely technical reversal.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – no definitive crash‑bottom signal and the company is still proving its operational model.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the plant starts delivering revenue as planned and the cash position improves, the shares could climb to the mid‑levels of the pre‑crash range, offering a 5‑10× upside relative to today’s price. The options grant also shows that insiders are willing to stake capital, which can be a bullish sign.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still carries significant debt and its revenue stream is not yet proven. A delay in construction or a failure to secure long‑term contracts could leave it with a higher burn rate and potential default risk.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 5–10× if the plant is successful; downside limited to the current price if the company fails to meet milestones. The asymmetry is modest compared to classic deep‑crash plays, so a cautious stance is advisable.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter only after a clear volume spike and a breakout above the recent 20‑day low. Allocate 5–10% of the portfolio, set a stop just below the breakout level, and trail the stop as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a small position (or a covered call) and watch for the March bond coupon payment, the private placement close, or a construction‑completion announcement. These events are the most likely triggers.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a significant debt restructuring, a liquidation of assets, or a regulatory halt to its projects, consider exiting immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Zenith Energy is at a rebuild stage; a clear operational milestone or cash‑flow boost could lift the shares 5‑10×, but the lack of a proven crash‑bottom signal and lingering debt keep the risk moderate.
